title: trifolium purpureum subsp. desvauxii
intro: Trifolium purpureum subsp. desvauxii, also known as Desvaux's purple clover, is a subspecies of the purple clover plant, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its high content of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Trifolium purpureum subsp. desvauxii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are used to support menopausal symptoms, improve digestion, and boost immune function.
